releases.title: v3.4.12
releases.summary: This release brings the event autograph linking contract into the Event drawer. Autograph artifacts can now capture signer details, show their event-side provenance, and link directly to canonical Art or media signature records from the UI.

releases.title: v3.4.11
releases.summary: This release connects event-captured autographs to the shared signature provenance model introduced for Art and media. Events remain the capture/history surface, while Art and media records remain the canonical signed-object evidence surface.

releases.title: v3.4.10
releases.summary: This release completes the next shared signature provenance slice by giving Art the same proof-image upload and removal path already available to media signing proofs. Art proof files now update the shared primary signature record directly, while media proof removal keeps the legacy media field and shared signature record aligned.

releases.title: v3.4.9
releases.summary: This release introduces one shared signature provenance contract for signed collection objects. Art now records richer signature details through the shared model, while existing media title signature fields continue to work as compatibility projections onto the same foundation.

releases.title: v3.4.8
releases.summary: This release gives Art and Collectibles a shared `Fandom / Franchise` metadata field so source, fandom, franchise, and universe context can live beside the item without becoming object taxonomy. Art medium/type and Collectibles category stay focused on what the object is, while fandom context becomes searchable metadata across both libraries.

releases.title: v3.4.7
releases.summary: This release makes Event-side tracked purchase management feel first-class now that Art and Collectibles share the same purchased-item relationship. Event details can search, link, read back, and edit tracked Art or Collectibles purchases without sending the user through each library first.

releases.title: v3.4.6
releases.summary: This release moves new Art writes off the bridge-era Collectibles write path. Art now creates, edits, uploads images, removes images, and archives records through native `art_items` first, while migrated Art records remain readable through their existing bridge-compatible IDs.

details.heading: Native Art writes
bullets: New `/api/art` creates now persist directly to `art_items` with `source_collectible_id = NULL`.
Art updates now apply to native `art_items` rows first.
Art image upload, image removal, and archive/delete flows no longer require a hidden Collectibles row for native-only Art.
Existing migrated Art rows with `source_collectible_id` remain editable and readable through the bridge-safe route ID.

details.heading: Bridge compatibility
bullets: `/api/art/:id` resolves native-only Art by native ID and migrated Art by `source_collectible_id`.
Public Art responses continue to include `native_art_id` and `source_collectible_id` so the UI can distinguish native-only and migrated records during the compatibility window.
The `source_collectible_id` column intentionally remains in place for migrated rows.

details.heading: Event purchase links
bullets: Art create/update flows preserve shared Event purchase linking through `event_purchased_items`.
Event purchase filters and readback continue to resolve Art through native `art_items.id`.
Collectibles remain excluded from Art writes and `/api/collectibles` continues to reject Art records.

details.heading: Backlog tracking
bullets: Added explicit backlog follow-ups for Art signature provenance, fandom/franchise metadata, Event purchase linking polish, and Collectibles naming review.

releases.title: v3.4.5
releases.summary: This release simplifies Collectibles taxonomy and gives Art the medium/type boundary needed for comic-panel-style artwork. Collectibles keeps one visible classification selector, while Art now captures medium/type and signed status directly.

releases.title: v3.4.4
releases.summary: This release finishes the product-layer Art split by giving Art its own frontend surface, reducing Collectibles-specific compatibility assumptions, and documenting the shared Event purchase relationship. Art remains bridge-safe for migrated rows and existing IDs, but users now interact with Art as its own object domain rather than as a Collectibles subtype.

releases.title: v3.4.3
releases.summary: This patch cuts the Art library read path over to native Art storage while keeping bridge-compatible response ids during the transition. Events now read tracked Art purchases and tracked Collectibles purchases through the shared purchased-item relationship, and the Art drawer treats vendor and booth as event purchase context instead of always-on Art identity fields.
